article.title: Apple Kicks Off Five-Device Launch Week With iPhone 17e, Budget MacBook, and Pro Chip Refresh
article.summary: Apple begins a week of staggered press-release announcements on March 2 targeting consumers, students, and creative professionals simultaneously.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Apple launched the first wave of what analysts expect to be its largest single-week hardware release since the original iPhone lineup, beginning Monday, March 2, with announcements due to continue through Wednesday, March 4. Rather than staging a traditional keynote, the company is relying on sequential press releases and an "Apple Experience" event in New York, London, and Shanghai on Wednesday where journalists and invited guests can handle the devices hands-on. Up to seven products are expected before the week ends, spanning the iPhone, iPad, and Mac families.

## What We Know

### iPhone 17e

The centrepiece of day one is the iPhone 17e, which [9to5Mac reports](https://9to5mac.com/2026/03/01/apple-is-launching-new-products-this-week-heres-whats-coming/) will carry Apple's latest A19 chip — the same silicon introduced in the iPhone 17 line last autumn. The device keeps the 6.1-inch OLED display at 60 Hz but gains Dynamic Island in place of the notch, along with MagSafe support and the C1-series modem that Apple debuted last year. The rear camera remains a single 48-megapixel sensor; the front camera steps up to 12 megapixels with Center Stage framing. Pricing is unchanged at $599, matching the iPhone 16e.

According to [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/newsletters/2026-02-08/apple-readies-iphone-17e-new-siri-entry-level-ipad-ipad-air-and-macbook-pro-mldr3hpk), Apple's internal roadmap also connects the device to a forthcoming update to Siri, described as a more capable, on-device personal assistant. That feature is not expected to ship with the hardware at launch but is tied to an upcoming software update later this year.

### New Budget MacBook

The week's most strategically novel product is an all-new entry-level MacBook — a form factor Apple retired years ago and has now revived to target PC and Chromebook switchers. As reported by [9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2026/03/01/apple-is-launching-new-products-this-week-heres-whats-coming/), the machine is built around the A18 Pro chip from the iPhone 16 Pro, paired with 8 GB of RAM — enough to run Apple Intelligence — and ships in a range of colours including light yellow, light green, blue, pink, silver, and dark grey.

The display is a 12.9-inch LCD panel, and the chassis uses aluminium construction. Connectivity is limited to USB-C without Thunderbolt. [Wccftech](https://www.wccftech.com/apple-ensures-that-its-retail-stores-can-handle-the-upcoming-frenzy-surrounding-the-low-cost-macbook-the-iphone-17e-and-the-updated-macbook-pro-ipad-and-ipad-air/) notes that Apple has briefed retail staff to expect demand comparable to an iPhone launch season, dedicating full display tables to the new MacBook — an unusual allocation for a non-iPhone product. Pricing is expected to land between $699 and $749.

### MacBook Pro With M5 Pro and M5 Max

Apple is also refreshing the 14-inch and 16-inch MacBook Pro lines with M5 Pro and M5 Max chips. No design changes are planned; the update is chip-for-chip performance improvement ahead of a more significant redesign anticipated with M6 later this year. [9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2026/03/01/apple-is-launching-new-products-this-week-heres-whats-coming/) notes Apple is marketing the new Pro models explicitly for AI workloads, reflecting the company's push to frame its Apple Silicon advantage in terms of on-device machine learning capability.

### iPad Refreshes

Two iPad updates are confirmed in pre-launch reporting. The base iPad gains an A18 chip upgrade, enabling the full suite of Apple Intelligence features and positioning it for the new Siri when that update ships. The iPad Air moves from M3 to M4, maintaining its 11-inch and 13-inch size options at their existing price points of $599 and $799 respectively, according to [9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2026/03/01/apple-is-launching-new-products-this-week-heres-whats-coming/).

### Possible Additional Releases

Beyond the confirmed five products, [9to5Mac](https://9to5mac.com/2026/03/01/apple-is-launching-new-products-this-week-heres-whats-coming/) reports that an M5 MacBook Air and an M5 Mac Studio — including an M5 Ultra variant — may also appear during the week, though timing is less certain. A new Studio Display with 120 Hz ProMotion and an A19-class chip has also been rumoured, though Apple has not confirmed it.

## What We Don't Know

Because Apple is using press releases rather than a livestreamed keynote, the exact cadence of announcements is unclear. The company has not confirmed which products are shipping immediately versus placed on order. It is also unknown whether a MacBook Air refresh will arrive this week or follow later in spring, as it did last year. Details on any software features exclusive to the new hardware — including capabilities tied to the promised Siri update — have not been publicly disclosed ahead of the formal launch.

## Analysis

Apple's decision to spread announcements across a week of press releases rather than a single event departs from years of practice and reflects a product cadence that has become too broad to fit a single-day stage presentation. The week bundles three distinct market segments — budget consumers with the iPhone 17e and entry MacBook, general productivity users with the iPad refreshes, and creative professionals with the MacBook Pro — suggesting the company is attempting to capture a wide market at the start of the calendar year before competitors' spring product cycles begin.

The budget MacBook is the most strategically significant product in the lineup. Apple abandoned the sub-$1,000 Mac laptop segment years ago, and its return signals renewed competitive pressure from Chromebook vendors and Microsoft's Surface range in education and emerging markets. Retail preparations described by [Wccftech](https://www.wccftech.com/apple-ensures-that-its-retail-stores-can-handle-the-upcoming-frenzy-surrounding-the-low-cost-macbook-the-iphone-17e-and-the-updated-macbook-pro-ipad-and-ipad-air/) — with stores bracing for iPhone-level foot traffic — indicate Apple expects high volume, though whether consumer appetite matches those internal expectations will not be clear until sales data emerges in the April earnings call.
